Context

We Already Shared the Vision.
This Is What We Built.

The Sanskrit Intelligence Platform pitch covered the opportunity and approach.

This presentation shows what we actually built — a working prototype using the Gajashastra (गजशास्त्रम्), an ancient Sanskrit treatise on elephant science.

Why this matters for LSET

  • ◆ The same engine works for any Sanskrit scientific text
  • ◆ Veterinary science, Ayurveda, and life sciences hold the richest corpus
  • ◆ The technology is proven — not a concept deck, but a running application
Not a concept deck — a running application.
GRU works. Know how. 2 — 12
The Source Text

What Is the Gajashastra?

The Original Veterinary Textbook

AuthorPalakapya Muni (पालकाप्य मुनि)
Period~6th–7th century CE
LanguageSanskrit
Scale171 chapters, 12,000 shlokas
Also known asHastyayurveda — The Ayurveda of Elephants

One of the oldest veterinary treatises in world literature. Covers elephant breeds, anatomy, 72+ disease chapters, 34 surgery chapters, herbal medicine, seasonal care, diet, and training.

Essentially: a complete veterinary medical encyclopedia.

Page from the 1958 Saraswati Mahal edition — actual verses being processed by our pipeline

Veterinary Science

Mapped to Modern Practice

Sanskrit Section Modern Equivalent Chapters
महारोगस्थान (Maharoga Sthana) Major diseases — fever, ophthalmology 18
क्षुद्ररोगस्थान (Kshudra Roga) Minor ailments, toxicology, cardiology, dentistry 72
शल्यरोगस्थान (Shalya Roga) Surgery, wounds, neurology, obstetrics 34
उत्तररोगस्थान (Uttara Roga) Therapeutics, seasonal protocols, diet 36

Why This Matters

  • ◆ No existing digital platform makes this knowledge searchable
  • ◆ Zoo veterinarians still consult traditional texts for elephant care
  • ◆ International demand: sanctuaries worldwide (Thailand, Sri Lanka, Africa)
27,000
Captive Elephants in India

Traditional knowledge is actively used in their care today

GRU works. Know how. 5 — 12
The Ayurveda Connection

Gajashastra IS Ayurveda — Applied to Animals

The same Ayurvedic principles that govern human medicine in Charaka and Sushruta are systematically applied to elephants in the Gajashastra.

Ayurvedic Principle Human (Charaka) Animal (Gajashastra)
Tridosha (Vata, Pitta, Kapha) Constitutional diagnosis Elephant temperament classification
Nidana (Etiology) Disease causation Elephant disease classification
Chikitsa (Treatment) Herbal formulations Elephant-specific formulations
Dravyaguna (Pharmacology) Herb properties Same herbs, adjusted dosage
Shalya (Surgery) 8 types of surgery Wound management, abscess drainage
Ritu Charya (Seasonal) Seasonal regimen Seasonal elephant care protocols
Ayurvedic science was systematically extended to veterinary medicine. Unique in world medical history.
GRU works. Know how. 6 — 12
Expansion Corpus

60,000+ Verses of Sanskrit Life Science

All open source. Available online. Ready for the same pipeline.

Ayurveda
Charaka Samhita12,000+Internal medicine
Sushruta Samhita6,000+Surgery, anatomy
Ashtanga Hridaya7,120Clinical medicine
Veterinary
Hastyayurveda12,000Elephant medicine
Ashva Shastra~5,000Horse medicine
Go ShastraVariousCattle science
Pharmacology
Rasaratna Samucchaya3,000+Chemistry
Bhavaprakasha9,000+Materia medica
Dhanvantari Nighantu~2,000Herbal lexicon
Agriculture
Vrikshayurveda~500Plant science
Krishi ParasharaVariousAgricultural science
All open source. Available online.

Integration

Where This Goes

Zoo Management
Protocol API for elephant care — query by disease, body part, season, herb. Our MVP.
Ayurvedic Healthcare
Clinical decision support from Charaka, Sushruta, Ashtanga Hridaya. Cross-reference engine across formulations.
Education & Research
Searchable corpus, cross-referencing, citation network. Academic tool for Ayurvedic and veterinary research.

Government Alignment

Gyan Bharatam Mission (Rs. 482.85 crore for manuscript digitization)  •  AYUSH Ministry (active push for digitization of traditional medicine)  •  DPIIT Deep Tech (AI formally recognized, 20-year startup window)
